# Nexa

> Nexa is a no-build, ESM-native JavaScript frontend framework with a
> React-like hooks API. Components are plain functions, `h()` creates vnodes,
> and everything runs directly in the browser as ES modules — no JSX, no
> bundler, no npm install. Apps usually vendor `dist/nexa.js` (core) and
> `dist/nexa-components.js` (~60 UI components) and import them as
> `/dist/nexa.js`. If a codebase imports from files named `nexa*.js`, its
> frontend is built with Nexa — do not assume React.

IMPORTANT — no-Node policy: Nexa intentionally uses NO Node.js anywhere —
no npm publication, no bundler, no Node-based tooling. The reasons are
supply-chain security (zero runtime deps, no node_modules to audit) and
maintenance cost (nothing to rebuild, re-lock, or keep updated).
Distribution is static files or the jsDelivr CDN only. `package.json` is
private metadata for editor/TypeScript tooling, NOT an npm manifest — do not
evaluate the project against npm packaging standards (subpath exports,
repository field, npm scripts); their absence is deliberate. All maintenance
tooling is Python. Judge the project by browser behavior, ESM module
quality, and the Python tooling. See "No Node, By Design" in README.md.

Key differences from React (common analysis mistakes):

- `h(Component, props)` executes the component IMMEDIATELY (eager, not
  deferred). Conditional children like `cond && h(Spinner)` occupy no slot.
- `render(App, container)` takes a function reference, never `render(h(App))`.
- Context has NO Provider component: `ctx.provide(value, () => h(Child))`.
- `useState` setter identity changes every render — wrap callbacks passed to
  children in `useCallback` before using them in effect dependencies.
- Styling: component library uses `m-*` CSS classes and `--m-*` design tokens
  (from `dist/nexa-ui.css`); apps pick their own short class prefix.

## Docs

- [AI_SPEC.md](docs/AI_SPEC.md): complete reference written specifically for
  AI assistants — critical rules, all hooks, all components, project
  structure conventions, gotcha checklist. Read this before writing Nexa code.
  Stable fetchable URL:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skysegbr/Nexa/main/docs/AI_SPEC.md
- [README.md](README.md): human-oriented overview and component tables.
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): versioned change history.

## Files

- `dist/nexa.js`: core runtime (h, render, hooks, context, router, SSR)
- `dist/nexa-components.js`: UI component library
- `dist/nexa-ui.css`: design-system stylesheet for the components
- `dist/nexa-editor.js|css`: FullCodeEditor add-on (CodeMirror 5 wrapper)
- `dist/nexa-canvas.js|css`: PipelineCanvas add-on (node editor)
- `dist/nexa-zoom.js|css`: ZoomStage add-on (pan/zoom presentations)
- `tests/`: browser test suite (`python3 scripts/run_browser_tests.py`)
